{"society_power": 5.0, "state_power": 7.0, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 1.0, "key_event": "Independence (1964), copper wealth, and centralizing state consolidation", "reasoning": "Independence, copper-funded fiscal resources, mine nationalization, and expanded emergency/security powers markedly strengthened the sovereign state, though a weak inherited administrative base kept it from the top tier. Society's nationalist mobilization and union growth were offset by suppression of the Lumpa Church, co-optation of unions, pressure on the opposition, and ethno-regional fragmentation, leaving societal power roughly flat."}
